---
title: "FW 536 — Day 1 morning lab (v3 · terrestrial wildlife & disease) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 1 morning lab — Probability problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — terrestrial wildlife & disease theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — marine & fisheries](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Lynx tracks on a snow transect

> A 5 km transect is divided into 50 segments of 100 m each. At a given snow depth and time of year, the probability of detecting at least one lynx track in any given segment is $p = 0.06$, independently.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — Avian influenza screening

> A flock of waterfowl has prior infection prevalence 0.08. A swab test has sensitivity 0.95 and specificity 0.90.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Wolf encounters at active dens

> An observer records wolf approaches to a den at an average rate of $\lambda = 1.8$ per 12-h observation period.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 4 — Bobcat camera grid heterogeneity

> Eight remote cameras are deployed for one week. Mean per-camera detection probability is $\bar p = 0.20$, but cameras vary widely in placement quality.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Songbird point counts

> At hermit thrush point counts, the average count is 2.4 birds, but ~25% of points are in unsuitable habitat where the true count is 0.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
